title: Return actual commit outcome to prevent false success claims
context: commitAndPushHunch had a void return type, so callers always reported success even when the commit was skipped (lock held, safety backstop refusal, empty stage) or push failed (offline/no upstream). MCP responses claimed 'auto-committed' or 'pushed' regardless of what actually happened. Touches invariant(s): con_8460b6770f (Config/provider writers must merge idempotently into existing user files and refuse to clobber an unparseable file); con_e04226bd05 (The MCP server must stay client-agnostic: all assistants point to the same .hunch/ graph; no Claude-only behavior in the server).
decision: Changed commitAndPushHunch return type from void to 'pushed' | 'committed' | null. Callers now receive truth: null = nothing happened, 'committed' = commit created but push deferred/failed, 'pushed' = actually pushed. MCP server only claims success that occurred. Skipped commits remain on disk for next flush (eventually-consistent retry).
consequences: Callers must decide what to report to users based on actual outcome, not assumption
User-facing messages now honest: skip says nothing, committed says 'rides next push', pushed says 'pushed'
flushNote() helper centralizes message logic to prevent future false claims
Test assertions now verify the function reports skips correctly (not as commits)
alternatives_rejected: Keep void, add separate status-check mechanism (complexity without solving the root issue of callers not knowing what happened)
Throw on failure (breaks the eventually-consistent retry pattern where failed commits are swept up on next flush)
Encode 'attempted' vs 'succeeded' in the message string itself (doesn't give callers semantic return value to key decisions on)

rejected_tripwires.alternative: Keep void, add separate status-check mechanism (complexity without solving the root issue of callers not knowing what happened)
Throw on failure (breaks the eventually-consistent retry pattern where failed commits are swept up on next flush)
Encode 'attempted' vs 'succeeded' in the message string itself (doesn't give callers semantic return value to key decisions on)
